opp.name: BernQ RNG Opp S2C Feb '26
opp.ais_why:
• On the Feb 20 intro, Giorgi explicitly required that AI features 'operate without using proprietary data for ongoing training' - open to AI but guarded.
• Interest centered on eSourcing, contract management and spend analysis; autonomous negotiation and Merlin agentic intake did not surface as priorities.
• Sage Intacct go-live in July pushes any Zycus deployment to year-end, so cadence is slow and AI urgency is low.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI target was confirmed by the buyer. On the 20 Feb 2026 call, Giorgi Iamanidze (Manager, Procurement) asked 'So if I pay 150k, do I save at least $1,000,000? The cost does not matter. What matters is the value.' This signals the buyer expects a clear value case, but Bailey Erazo deferred to a future business-case build ('more than happy to build that out for you later down the road'). No specific pain metric — cost, headcount, cycle time, error rate — was quantified by the buyer. The gap is Giorgi never stated a current cost baseline, procurement cycle time, or savings target; the deal team must quantify the operational pain (manual RFP/contract creation, lack of spend visibility across three procurement stages) and return with a concrete ROI model tied to the buyer's 100+ POs/month volume and three-stage procurement complexity.

sources: Engagement pulse: DARK, 126 days since last contact
champion.narrative: Giorgi Iamanidze (Manager, Procurement) is a developing champion but has gone silent for 126 days. On the 20 Feb call, Giorgi demonstrated genuine interest: he asked detailed questions about AI training on proprietary data, ERP integration, 3-way matching, spend visibility by procurement stage, and explicitly stated the value-first mindset ('the cost does not matter, what matters is the value... if I pay 150k do I save at least $1M?'). He confirmed he has an assistant (implying he leads a procurement function), stated he would build the internal business case ('I will see the value, then present to upper management'), and requested demo videos, slide decks, and cost breakdown to review in March 2026. However, Giorgi has NO access to the Economic Buyer (he must 'present to upper management'), and he deferred engagement by 3+ months ('I will have time to look into it in March', call 20 Feb). Since that call 126 days ago, there has been zero buyer engagement, no reply to the materials Zycus committed to send, and no follow-up meeting scheduled. Giorgi is at risk of cooling or deprioritizing Zycus entirely. His current engagement status is passive/cooling. The deal team must re-engage Giorgi immediately, confirm he reviewed the materials, rebuild momentum, and secure an introduction to the Economic Buyer and the broader evaluation committee (legal, IT, project managers) to move from developing to active champion.

competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. No competitor was named by Giorgi Iamanidze on the 20 Feb call, and the Salesforce Competitors__c field is blank. Giorgi stated BerQ RNG currently uses ChatGPT and Excel for RFP creation, and Sage Intacct for ERP (go-live July 2026), but no procurement platform or competitive vendor was mentioned. The deal appears to be a greenfield 'do nothing' vs 'buy Zycus' evaluation rather than a competitive bake-off, but this is unconfirmed — Giorgi's statement 'I will have to present the value to upper management' implies he may be evaluating alternatives or building a business case that includes a status-quo (continue with ChatGPT/Excel/Sage Intacct only) option. The risk is that upper management may decide the Sage Intacct native procurement capabilities (if any) are sufficient, or that another vendor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP) is already being evaluated in parallel without Zycus visibility. The deal team must ask Giorgi directly on re-engagement: 'Are you evaluating other procurement platforms alongside Zycus, or is this a build-vs-buy decision internally?' and surface any competitive or status-quo pressure early.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and specific to BerQ RNG's three-stage procurement model (development, construction, operations). Giorgi Iamanidze articulated the following operational pains on the 20 Feb call: (1) manual RFP creation using ChatGPT and Excel ('we are using ChatGPT... we do have a couple of subscriptions'), which Zycus claims takes 40 days on average without procurement software; (2) lack of spend visibility across the three procurement stages ('spend analysis will be very interesting... I need to see the spend per level, per item, per contract', call 20 Feb); (3) manual PO data entry ('there are many line items and it takes time', call 20 Feb); (4) contract management complexity across development and construction stages ('contract management will be helpful as well, especially the development and construction stage because those are stages when we do the contracting', call 20 Feb); (5) limited supplier risk visibility (acknowledged as partially valuable given sole-source constraints, call 20 Feb). The pain owner is Giorgi (Manager, Procurement), but the broader pain likely affects project managers (who need contract visibility) and legal (who need contract repository/redlining). The pain is tied to a 100+ PO/month volume plus separate GC contracts and service contracts across multiple plants in Canada and the US. The compelling event is the Sage Intacct go-live in July 2026, after which BerQ RNG can implement an integrated procurement platform; missing is the quantified cost of the current pain (hours spent per RFP, procurement cycle time, maverick spend, contract leakage).

sources: Avoma call 20 Feb 2026, Giorgi Iamanidze (legal team mention only, no process detail)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process is unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal redlining, procurement channel, InfoSec review, or contracting mechanics occurred on the 20 Feb call. Giorgi mentioned legal team involvement ('our legal team should be there' for contract management module access, call 20 Feb), confirming legal is a stakeholder, but no legal contact has been introduced and no InfoSec or data-privacy review process was discussed despite Giorgi's explicit concern about AI training on proprietary data ('is this platform using this information to train its AI, and how is my information saved within this platform?', call 20 Feb). The Sage Intacct ERP implementation (go-live July 2026) is the forcing function for timing, but no contracting timeline, approval steps, or partner involvement (SI, Sage partner) was confirmed. The deal team must surface the paper process: does BerQ RNG require an MSA review, what is the InfoSec questionnaire and approval timeline, does the Sage implementation partner (if any) need to be looped in for integration scoping, and what is the legal review cycle for a $150k SaaS contract.

sources: Account contacts: Giorgi Iamanidze (Manager Procurement), Collin Ng (Manager IT)
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unidentified. Giorgi Iamanidze (Manager, Procurement) stated on the 20 Feb call 'I will go through it, I will see the value, then I will have to present the value to our upper management... the decision makers are management, of course.' This confirms Giorgi is NOT the Economic Buyer — he is an evaluator and internal champion-candidate who must present upward. The EB is 'upper management' (title and name unknown). No executive (CFO, CPO, VP Operations) has joined a call, been named in a contact role, or appeared in any task. The only account-level contacts on record are Giorgi (Procurement) and Collin Ng (IT Manager), neither with budget authority. The deal team must map the EB (likely VP Finance, CFO, or COO given the $150k spend level and three-stage procurement model across development/construction/operations), secure an introduction from Giorgi, and run a value workshop with that executive before any forecast is defensible.

decision_process.narrative: Decision process is partially mapped. Giorgi Iamanidze (Manager, Procurement) is the initial evaluator and must build an internal business case for upper management approval ('I will see the value, then I will have to present the value to our upper management... the decision makers are management', call 20 Feb). The approval chain runs: Giorgi evaluates → presents value case to upper management (titles/names unknown) → management decides. Timeline dependency: Sage Intacct ERP go-live is July 2026 ('we are in the middle of implementing Sage... go live date is July', call 20 Feb), placing any Zycus deployment at 'end of the year, early next year' (Giorgi, call 20 Feb). Giorgi also stated he would review materials in March 2026, but no follow-up occurred. Missing: who is on the evaluation committee beyond Giorgi, whether legal/IT/finance run parallel reviews, whether a formal RFP/RFI process is required, whether reference customers or a POC are part of the process, and the approval threshold for a $150k spend. The deal is single-threaded (zero contact roles, one buyer on one call) with no visibility into the executive decision-making layer. The deal team must map the full committee, confirm the post-July deployment window, and secure a multi-stakeholder discovery session that includes the Economic Buyer and legal/IT leads.

decision_criteria.narrative: Partial criteria emerged from the 20 Feb discovery call but remain incomplete. Giorgi Iamanidze stated explicit requirements: (1) ERP integration via open APIs with the relevant stakeholder ('requisitions and POs in Zycus must sync to the relevant stakeholder', call 20 Feb); (2) spend visibility by procurement stage (development, construction, operations) and by item/contract ('require spend metrics by item and by contract... spend visibility by procurement stage: development, construction, operations', call 20 Feb); (3) AI functionalities that do NOT use proprietary data for training ('the AI model is not training on our information... do you have a similar option with your software?', confirmed by Bailey as available, call 20 Feb); (4) RFP/RFX automation (currently using ChatGPT/Excel, 40-day cycle per Zycus claim); (5) contract repository for development and construction stages; (6) supplier risk monitoring (acknowledged as '50/50 value' due to sole-source major equipment suppliers, call 20 Feb). Missing: formal evaluation scorecard, decision timeline, competitive evaluation process, reference-customer requirement, InfoSec/legal review process. The deal team must return with a fit matrix showing how Zycus meets the stated criteria and surface any unstated decision factors (e.g. implementation timeline post-Sage go-live in July 2026, user adoption across 20+ legal/PM/procurement users, partner/SI involvement).

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI Curious. Giorgi Iamanidze explicitly asked about AI for procurement and raised a data-privacy concern, signaling interest in AI capabilities but also caution about proprietary data use. Bailey confirmed Zycus offers AI functionalities (RFP creation, contract scanning, PO automation, machine learning) with an opt-out for training on customer data, which satisfied Giorgi's concern ('Okay, understood'). However, no AIS score, status, or why-text is populated in Salesforce, so AI appetite is unquantified by the seller. The opportunity includes 'ANA' (AI/analytics) in the Products field, indicating AI is in scope. Giorgi's behavior (asking about AI, accepting the opt-out answer, requesting demos of AI-powered modules) places him in the AI Curious tier: interested in AI value but not leading with an AI-first strategy. The deal team should position Merlin and intelligent automation as the wedge to automate the three-stage procurement model and deliver quantified ROI, elevating AI from feature to strategic differentiator.

confidence_signals.summary: Confidence is very low. The deal is 126 days dark with zero buyer engagement since a single introductory call on 20 Feb 2026. Giorgi Iamanidze deferred next steps to March 2026 (now 3+ months overdue), and there is no evidence he reviewed the materials Zycus committed to send. Zero contact roles, no multi-threading, no Economic Buyer identification, no quantified ROI case, no second meeting, no competitive intel, and no forward progression. The deal is effectively a ghost. Cooling signals: 126-day silence, buyer deferral with no follow-up, single-threaded, no EB mapped, no value case delivered despite buyer's explicit ask.

The buyer stated on the 20 Feb call they would review materials in March 2026 (~3 months ago), but no follow-up occurred. Zero forward movement in over 4 months forces Off Track.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal is critically at risk due to 126 days of silence following a single introductory call, zero multi-threading, no Economic Buyer identification, and no quantified value case. The buyer explicitly deferred engagement to March 2026 (now 3 months overdue) and has not replied to committed materials. Without immediate re-engagement, executive alignment, and a concrete ROI model tied to the buyer's $1M savings bar, this deal will remain stalled indefinitely.

competitive_position.summary: No named competitor. The deal appears to be a greenfield 'do nothing' (status quo: ChatGPT + Excel for RFPs, Sage Intacct for ERP) vs 'buy Zycus' evaluation. Giorgi Iamanidze confirmed on the 20 Feb call that BerQ RNG currently uses ChatGPT and Excel for RFP creation and is mid-implementation of Sage Intacct ERP (go-live July 2026). No procurement platform or competitive vendor was mentioned. The primary threat is STATUS QUO / DO NOTHING: upper management may decide Sage Intacct's native procurement features (if any) are sufficient, or may deprioritize the investment given the Sage implementation workload. The secondary unknown threat is a parallel evaluation Zycus is not aware of — Giorgi must build a value case for 'upper management', and it is unclear whether he is evaluating other vendors (Coupa, Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP) concurrently. Until Zycus re-engages and surfaces competitive pressure, the assumed competition is inertia and the Sage Intacct default.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is weak and underdeveloped. Giorgi Iamanidze asked explicitly about AI for procurement on the 20 Feb call ('You are even offering the AI for procurement, right?') and raised a critical concern about proprietary data use for AI training ('Is this platform using this information to train its AI?'). Bailey Erazo confirmed Zycus AI functionalities (RFP creation, contract analysis, PO automation, machine learning) and the opt-out capability, but did NOT position Zycus as differentiated on AI or highlight Merlin/agentic capabilities. The conversation treated AI as a feature-check, not a strategic advantage. Given the Products field includes 'ANA' (AI/analytics) and Giorgi's explicit AI interest, Zycus missed an opportunity to lead with the AI narrative and differentiate on intelligent automation vs manual ChatGPT/Excel workflows. The deal team must return with a Merlin-led value story showing how Zycus AI automates the three-stage procurement model (development, construction, operations) and delivers the $1M+ ROI Giorgi demanded.

items.criterion: AI functionalities that do NOT use proprietary data for ongoing training

items.quote: The cost does not matter. What matters is the value. If I pay 150k do I save at least $1M? So what value can you give me? (Giorgi Iamanidze)... More than happy to build that out for you later down the road (Bailey Erazo)

items.criterion: Quantified ROI and value case ($1M+ savings for $150k investment)

items.criterion: Sandbox or trial environment for hands-on evaluation

items.quote: 3-way matching is done in Sage Intacct... invoicing is done in Sage, I would say there's no need (Bailey Erazo and Giorgi agreement)
items.source: Avoma call 20 Feb 2026, Giorgi Iamanidze and Bailey Erazo

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Partial fit confirmed on key requirements, but significant gaps remain unaddressed. Zycus aligns on: (1) Sage Intacct integration via open APIs (confirmed by Bailey Erazo on call 20 Feb); (2) spend visibility by procurement stage, item, and contract (Zycus spend analysis module matches Giorgi's stated need, call 20 Feb); (3) AI functionality with opt-out of proprietary data training (Bailey confirmed 'you can turn it off if you don't want it', call 20 Feb); (4) modular platform for eSourcing, contract management, supplier info management, eProcurement (Giorgi expressed interest in all except eInvoicing, call 20 Feb). Exposure: (1) no quantified ROI case provided despite Giorgi's explicit ask for value proof ('if I pay 150k do I save at least $1M?'); (2) no business case or cost breakdown sent despite commitment to send 'this afternoon' 20 Feb (no evidence of follow-up); (3) no sandbox/trial environment available (Giorgi asked, Bailey said no, call 20 Feb); (4) eInvoicing module does not fit BerQ RNG's 3-way matching in Sage Intacct (Giorgi confirmed 'invoicing is done in Sage, no need', call 20 Feb). The deal is vulnerable on value quantification and lack of trial/hands-on evaluation.

hard.ais_why:
• On the Feb 20 intro, Giorgi explicitly required that AI features 'operate without using proprietary data for ongoing training' - open to AI but guarded.
• Interest centered on eSourcing, contract management and spend analysis; autonomous negotiation and Merlin agentic intake did not surface as priorities.
• Sage Intacct go-live in July pushes any Zycus deployment to year-end, so cadence is slow and AI urgency is low.

pulse.summary: Dark: no verified buyer engagement in 126 day(s) (last 2026-02-20).

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply
